Member-only story

After years of trial and error, these are the layout tricks I use on almost every project

4 min read
1 hour ago

--

A few years ago, around 1:20 AM, I was staring at a webpage that looked perfectly aligned in Chrome.
I opened the same page on my phone.
Everything exploded.
Buttons overlapped each other. Cards escaped their containers. A sidebar decided it wanted to become a footer. I remember pushing my chair back and saying, “CSS… why?”
The funny part?
It wasn’t CSS.
It was me.
Like most developers, I kept trying to force layouts into doing what I wanted instead of understanding how browsers actually think. I was treating CSS like a collection of magic properties rather than a layout system.
That single night completely changed how I write CSS.
Over the last four years of building web applications, dashboards, landing pages, and internal tools, I’ve thrown away dozens of layout habits that only made my code harder to maintain.
Today, there are only a handful of layout techniques I reach for repeatedly.
